---
title: "Vasicek Model Calibration and Validation"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.




Vasicek Model Calibration and Validation in R

This script:
1. Simulates historical portfolio default data under a
   Vasicek one-factor credit risk model
2. Calibrates model parameters (PD and asset correlation)
   using maximum likelihood estimation
3. Performs parameter stability analysis
4. Performs sensitivity testing
5. Simulates correlated defaults and portfolio loss dynamics
6. Documents model assumptions and limitations



```{r}
rm(list = ls())
set.seed(123)
```




**1. Helper functions**

```{r}
# Conditional default probability under Vasicek one-factor model
# p(z) = Phi((Phi^{-1}(pd) - sqrt(rho)*z) / sqrt(1-rho))
conditional_pd <- function(z, pd, rho) {
  q <- qnorm(pd)
  pnorm((q - sqrt(rho) * z) / sqrt(1 - rho))
}

```

```{r}
# Simulate default counts across multiple periods
simulate_default_history <- function(T_periods, n_obligors, pd, rho) {
  Z <- rnorm(T_periods)  # systematic factor for each period
  default_counts <- numeric(T_periods)
  default_rates  <- numeric(T_periods)
  
  for (t in 1:T_periods) {
    p_t <- conditional_pd(Z[t], pd, rho)
    defaults_t <- rbinom(1, size = n_obligors, prob = p_t)
    default_counts[t] <- defaults_t
    default_rates[t]  <- defaults_t / n_obligors
  }
  
  data.frame(
    period = 1:T_periods,
    Z = Z,
    default_count = default_counts,
    default_rate = default_rates
  )
}
```


```{r}
# Negative log-likelihood for observed default counts
# We integrate over the latent systematic factor Z ~ N(0,1)
negloglik_vasicek <- function(param, default_counts, n_obligors) {
  pd  <- param[1]
  rho <- param[2]
  
  # parameter constraints
  if (pd <= 0.0001 || pd >= 0.30 || rho <= 0.0001 || rho >= 0.999) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  # marginal likelihood for each period:
  # L_t = integral Binomial(k_t | n, p(z)) * phi(z) dz
  # approximate with numerical integration
  ll_vec <- numeric(length(default_counts))
  
  for (t in seq_along(default_counts)) {
    k_t <- default_counts[t]
    
    integrand <- function(z) {
      p_t <- conditional_pd(z, pd, rho)
      dbinom(k_t, size = n_obligors, prob = p_t) * dnorm(z)
    }
    
    val <- try(
      integrate(integrand, lower = -8, upper = 8, subdivisions = 1000L)$value,
      silent = TRUE
    )
    
    if (inherits(val, "try-error") || is.na(val) || val <= 0) {
      return(1e10)
    }
    
    ll_vec[t] <- log(val)
  }
  
  return(-sum(ll_vec))
}

```


```{r}
# Calibrate PD and rho by MLE
fit_vasicek_mle <- function(default_counts, n_obligors,
                            init_pd = 0.03, init_rho = 0.10) {
  fit <- optim(
    par = c(init_pd, init_rho),
    fn = negloglik_vasicek,
    default_counts = default_counts,
    n_obligors = n_obligors,
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    lower = c(0.0001, 0.0001),
    upper = c(0.30, 0.999)
  )
  
  list(
    fit = fit,
    pd_hat = fit$par[1],
    rho_hat = fit$par[2],
    negloglik = fit$value,
    convergence = fit$convergence
  )
}
```



```{r}
# Simulate portfolio-level correlated defaults using estimated parameters
simulate_portfolio_loss <- function(n_scenarios, n_obligors, pd, rho,
                                    ead = 100, lgd = 0.45) {
  default_threshold <- qnorm(pd)
  default_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = n_scenarios, ncol = n_obligors)
  
  Z <- rnorm(n_scenarios)
  
  for (s in 1:n_scenarios) {
    epsilon <- rnorm(n_obligors)
    asset_return <- sqrt(rho) * Z[s] + sqrt(1 - rho) * epsilon
    default_matrix[s, ] <- ifelse(asset_return < default_threshold, 1, 0)
  }
  
  portfolio_default_rate <- rowMeans(default_matrix)
  portfolio_loss <- rowSums(default_matrix * ead * lgd)
  
  list(
    default_matrix = default_matrix,
    portfolio_default_rate = portfolio_default_rate,
    portfolio_loss = portfolio_loss
  )
}
```







**2. Simulate "historical" default data**
```{r}
# True model parameters
pd_true  <- 0.03
rho_true <- 0.15

# Historical panel setup
T_periods  <- 40      # e.g., 40 quarterly observations
n_obligors <- 500     # portfolio size per period

history_data <- simulate_default_history(
  T_periods = T_periods,
  n_obligors = n_obligors,
  pd = pd_true,
  rho = rho_true
)

cat("==== Simulated Historical Default Data (first 10 rows) ====\n")
print(head(history_data, 10))

```






**3. Maximum likelihood calibration**
```{r}
mle_result <- fit_vasicek_mle(
  default_counts = history_data$default_count,
  n_obligors = n_obligors,
  init_pd = 0.02,
  init_rho = 0.10
)

cat("\n==== MLE Calibration Results ====\n")
cat("Convergence code:", mle_result$convergence, "\n")
cat("Estimated PD :", round(mle_result$pd_hat, 6), "\n")
cat("Estimated rho:", round(mle_result$rho_hat, 6), "\n")
cat("Negative log-likelihood:", round(mle_result$negloglik, 4), "\n")

comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("PD", "rho"),
  True = c(pd_true, rho_true),
  Estimated = c(mle_result$pd_hat, mle_result$rho_hat),
  Error = c(mle_result$pd_hat - pd_true, mle_result$rho_hat - rho_true)
)

cat("\n==== Parameter Recovery Check ====\n")
print(comparison_table)
```







**4. Parameter stability analysis**

```{r}
# Split the time series into two subsamples
split_point <- floor(T_periods / 2)

hist_1 <- history_data$default_count[1:split_point]
hist_2 <- history_data$default_count[(split_point + 1):T_periods]

mle_1 <- fit_vasicek_mle(hist_1, n_obligors, init_pd = 0.02, init_rho = 0.10)
mle_2 <- fit_vasicek_mle(hist_2, n_obligors, init_pd = 0.02, init_rho = 0.10)

stability_table <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("PD", "rho"),
  Full_Sample = c(mle_result$pd_hat, mle_result$rho_hat),
  First_Half = c(mle_1$pd_hat, mle_1$rho_hat),
  Second_Half = c(mle_2$pd_hat, mle_2$rho_hat)
)

cat("\n==== Parameter Stability Analysis ====\n")
print(stability_table)
```




**5. Sensitivity testing**
```{r}
# Sensitivity of log-likelihood to rho around calibrated estimate
rho_grid <- seq(max(0.01, mle_result$rho_hat - 0.08),
                min(0.50, mle_result$rho_hat + 0.08),
                by = 0.02)

sensitivity_results <- data.frame(
  rho = rho_grid,
  NegLogLik = NA_real_
)

for (i in seq_along(rho_grid)) {
  test_param <- c(mle_result$pd_hat, rho_grid[i])
  sensitivity_results$NegLogLik[i] <- negloglik_vasicek(
    test_param,
    default_counts = history_data$default_count,
    n_obligors = n_obligors
  )
}

cat("\n==== Sensitivity Testing (rho) ====\n")
print(sensitivity_results)
```






**6. Simulate correlated default structures**
```{r}
portfolio_sim <- simulate_portfolio_loss(
  n_scenarios = 5000,
  n_obligors = 1000,
  pd = mle_result$pd_hat,
  rho = mle_result$rho_hat,
  ead = 100,
  lgd = 0.45
)

cat("\n==== Portfolio Default Rate Summary ====\n")
print(summary(portfolio_sim$portfolio_default_rate))

cat("\n==== Portfolio Loss Summary ====\n")
print(summary(portfolio_sim$portfolio_loss))

# Approximate pairwise default correlation
sample_cols <- 1:20
corr_mat <- cor(portfolio_sim$default_matrix[, sample_cols])
pairwise_corr <- corr_mat[upper.tri(corr_mat)]

cat("\n==== Approximate Pairwise Default Correlation ====\n")
print(mean(pairwise_corr, na.rm = TRUE))
```



**7. Stress / scenario analysis**
```{r}
stress_Z <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)

stress_table <- data.frame(
  Systematic_Factor = stress_Z,
  Conditional_PD = NA_real_
)

for (i in seq_along(stress_Z)) {
  stress_table$Conditional_PD[i] <- conditional_pd(
    z = stress_Z[i],
    pd = mle_result$pd_hat,
    rho = mle_result$rho_hat
  )
}

cat("\n==== Conditional PD under Stress Scenarios ====\n")
print(stress_table)

```




**8. Basic risk metrics**
```{r}
loss_var_95 <- quantile(portfolio_sim$portfolio_loss, 0.95)
loss_var_99 <- quantile(portfolio_sim$portfolio_loss, 0.99)

cat("\n==== Portfolio Loss Risk Metrics ====\n")
cat("VaR 95%:", round(loss_var_95, 2), "\n")
cat("VaR 99%:", round(loss_var_99, 2), "\n")
```





**9.Plots**
```{r}


plot(
  history_data$period, history_data$default_rate,
  type = "b",
  main = "Historical Default Rates",
  xlab = "Period",
  ylab = "Default Rate"
)

plot(
  sensitivity_results$rho, sensitivity_results$NegLogLik,
  type = "b",
  main = "Sensitivity of NegLogLik to rho",
  xlab = "rho",
  ylab = "Negative Log-Likelihood"
)

hist(
  portfolio_sim$portfolio_default_rate,
  breaks = 40,
  main = "Portfolio Default Rate Distribution",
  xlab = "Default Rate"
)

hist(
  portfolio_sim$portfolio_loss,
  breaks = 40,
  main = "Portfolio Loss Distribution",
  xlab = "Portfolio Loss"
)


```





**10. Assumptions and limitations**
```{r}
cat("\n==== Model Assumptions and Limitations ====\n")
cat("Assumptions:\n")
cat("- Homogeneous portfolio: same PD, same EAD, same LGD, same correlation.\n")
cat("- One-factor dependence structure through a single systematic risk factor.\n")
cat("- Conditional defaults are independent given the systematic factor.\n")
cat("- Historical periods are treated as independent draws of the systematic factor.\n\n")

cat("Limitations:\n")
cat("- This is a simplified portfolio credit model and does not include rating migration.\n")
cat("- LGD and EAD are assumed fixed rather than stochastic.\n")
cat("- The model does not include macroeconomic covariates or IFRS 9 lifetime ECL framework.\n")
cat("- Numerical integration is used for MLE and may be computationally expensive for larger datasets.\n")

```
